stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Faith Ringgold is an American artist known for making many story quilts.
  • A story quilt is a pieced fabric work that incorporates handwritten or stenciled text panels alongside painted imagery.
  • When viewed, the text panels narrate a story while the imagery illustrates the setting and characters.
  • Her story quilt Tar Beach (1988) depicts a Harlem rooftop scene viewed from above.
  • It is painted in acrylic on canvas with hand-dyed and pieced cotton borders.
stem: The student wants to describe Ringgold's Tar Beach to an audience already familiar with story quilts. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Depicting a Harlem rooftop scene viewed from above, the 1988 work is one of Ringgold's many story quilts, which are pieced fabric works that combine handwritten or stenciled text panels with painted imagery.
Known for making many other pieced fabric works called story quilts, Ringgold created Tar Beach in 1988.
Ringgold's 1988 story quilt Tar Beach depicts a Harlem rooftop scene viewed from above, painted in acrylic on canvas with hand-dyed and pieced cotton borders.
A story quilt, such as Ringgold's Tar Beach, is a pieced fabric work whose text panels narrate a story while its imagery illustrates the setting and characters.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence describes Tar Beach in a way that assumes the audience is already familiar with story quilts, focusing on the specific features of the work — its content and medium.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence defines what story quilts are; a familiar audience would not need that definition. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence mentions the year and the type of work but not the content or medium of Tar Beach, and it also redundantly defines story quilts for a familiar audience. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence provides a definition of story quilts, which is inappropriate for an audience already familiar with them.
